links.title: SAGE: Scalable Automated Robustness Augmentation for LLM Knowledge Evaluation

primary_link.title: SAGE: Scalable Automated Robustness Augmentation for LLM Knowledge Evaluation

papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong performance on standard knowledge evaluation benchmarks, yet recent work shows that their knowledge capabilities remain brittle under question variants that test the same knowledge in different forms. Robustness augmentation of existing knowledge evaluation benchmarks is therefore necessary, but current LLM-assisted generate-then-verify pipelines are costly and difficult to scale due to low-yield variant generation and unreliable variant verification.
papers.summary_zh: Large Language Models (LLMs) 在標準知識評估 benchmark 上取得了優異的性能，但最近的研究表明，在以不同形式測試相同知識的問題變體下，它們的知識能力仍然脆弱。因此，對現有知識評估 benchmark 進行 robustness augmentation 是必要的，但當前的 LLM-assisted generate-then-verify pipeline 由於低產量的變體生成和不可靠的變體驗證，成本高昂且難以擴展。
papers.title: SAGE: Scalable Automated Robustness Augmentation for LLM Knowledge Evaluation
papers.title_zh: SAGE：用於 LLM 知識評估的可擴展自動魯棒性增強

links.title: The Illusion of Power Capping in LLM Decode: A Phase-Aware Energy Characterisation Across Attention Architectures

primary_link.title: The Illusion of Power Capping in LLM Decode: A Phase-Aware Energy Characterisation Across Attention Architectures

papers.summary: Power capping is the standard GPU energy lever in LLM serving, and it appears to work: throughput drops, power readings fall, and energy budgets are met. We show the appearance is illusory for the phase that dominates production serving: autoregressive decode. Across four attention paradigms -- GQA, MLA, Gated DeltaNet, and Mamba2 -- on NVIDIA H200, decode draws only 137--300\,W on a 700\,W GPU; no cap ever triggers, because memory-bound decode saturates HBM bandwidth rather than compute and lea
papers.summary_zh: Power capping 是 LLM serving 中標準的 GPU 能源控制手段，它似乎有效：throughput 下降，功耗讀數降低，並且滿足了 energy budgets。我們證明，對於主導生產 serving 的階段：autoregressive decode，這種表象是虛幻的。在 NVIDIA H200 上，跨四種 attention paradigm——GQA、MLA、Gated DeltaNet 和 Mamba2——解碼僅消耗 137–300\,W，而 GPU 為 700\,W；沒有任何 cap 會被觸發，因為 memory-bound decode 飽和的是 HBM bandwidth 而非 compute 和 lea。

papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are trained for downstream tasks by updating their parameters (e.g., via RL). However, updating parameters forces them to absorb task-specific information, which can result in catastrophic forgetting and loss of plasticity. papers.summary: Large language models accumulate extensive parametric knowledge through pre-training. radar.summary_zh: 使用 large language models (LLMs) 自動生成教育材料正變得越來越普遍，但為這些材料分配難度級別仍需要大量人力。因此，LLM-as-a-Judge 引起了關注，然而與人類評分者的分歧仍是一個主要挑戰。我們提出了一種方法，用於預測哪些 LLM 生成的難度評級可能與人類評分者存在分歧，以便將這些情況送去重新評級。與先前方法不同的是
radar.title: Predicting Disagreement with Human Raters in LLM-as-a-Judge Difficulty Assessment without Using Generation-Time Probability Signals
radar.title_zh: 在不使用生成時機率訊號的情況下，預測 LLM-as-a-Judge 難度評估中與人類評分者的分歧

links.title: Assessing and Mitigating Miscalibration in LLM-Based Social Science Measurement

primary_link.title: Assessing and Mitigating Miscalibration in LLM-Based Social Science Measurement

radar.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used in social science as scalable measurement tools for converting unstructured text into variables that can enter standard empirical designs. radar.summary_zh: Large language models (LLMs) 在社會科學中越來越多地被用作可擴展的測量工具，用於將非結構化文本轉換為可納入標準實證設計的變數。測量有效性不僅要求高平均準確度，還需要良好校準的置信度，忠實反映每次測量正確的經驗機率。本文研究了基於 LLM 的社會科學測量中的模型校準不良問題。我們首先從對 FOMC 的案例研究開始
radar.title: Assessing and Mitigating Miscalibration in LLM-Based Social Science Measurement
radar.title_zh: 評估和緩解基於 LLM 的社會科學測量中的校準不良問題

links.title: Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement via Test-time LLM Guidance

primary_link.title: Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement via Test-time LLM Guidance

radar.summary: We explore the effectiveness of an LLM-guided query refinement paradigm for extending the usability of embedding models to challenging zero-shot search and classification tasks. Our approach refines the embedding representation of a user query using feedback from a generative LLM on a small set of documents, enabling embeddings to adapt in real time to the target task. radar.summary_zh: 我們探索了由 LLM 引導的查詢精煉範式在將 `embedding models` 的可用性擴展到具挑戰性的 `zero-shot search` 和 `classification tasks` 方面的有效性。我們的方法是利用來自 `generative LLM` 關於一小部分文件的反饋，來精煉用戶查詢的 `embedding representation`，使 `embeddings` 能夠實時適應目標任務。我們對多種具挑戰性的搜尋和分類任務，使用最先進的 `text embedding models` 進行了廣泛的實驗。
radar.title: Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement via Test-time LLM Guidance
radar.title_zh: 任務自適應的嵌入精煉：透過測試時的 LLM 引導

links.title: Attacks and Mitigations for Distributed Governance of Agentic AI under Byzantine Adversaries

primary_link.title: Attacks and Mitigations for Distributed Governance of Agentic AI under Byzantine Adversaries

radar.summary: Agentic AI governance is a critical component of agentic AI infrastructure ensuring that agents follow their owner's communication and interaction policies, and providing protection against attacks from malicious agents. The state-of-the-art solution, SAGA, assumes a logically centralized point of trust, the Provider, which serves as a repository for user and agent information and actively enforces policies. radar.summary_zh: `Agentic AI governance` 是 `agentic AI infrastructure` 的關鍵組成部分，它確保 `agents` 遵循其所有者的通訊和互動策略，並提供針對惡意 `agents` 攻擊的保護。最先進的解決方案 `SAGA` 假定了一個邏輯上集中的信任點，即 `Provider`，它作為用戶和 `agent` 資訊的儲存庫並主動執行策略。儘管 `SAGA` 提供了針對惡意 `agents` 的保護，但它仍然容易受到惡意攻擊。
radar.title: Attacks and Mitigations for Distributed Governance of Agentic AI under Byzantine Adversaries
radar.title_zh: 拜占庭式對手下智能體 AI 分散式治理的攻擊與緩解措施

links.title: Every Bit, Everywhere, All at Once: A Binomial Multibit LLM Watermark

primary_link.title: Every Bit, Everywhere, All at Once: A Binomial Multibit LLM Watermark

radar.summary: With LLM watermarking already being deployed commercially, practical applications increasingly require multibit watermarks that encode more complex payloads, such as user IDs or timestamps, into the generated text. radar.summary_zh: 隨著 `LLM watermarking` 已在商業上部署，實際應用越來越需要能夠將更複雜的 `payloads`（例如 `user IDs` 或 `timestamps`）編碼到生成文本中的 `multibit watermarks`。在這項工作中，我們提出了一種全新的 `multibit watermarking` 方法：引入 `binomial encoding`，以直接在每個 `token position` 編碼 `payload` 的每個位元。我們還通過一個 `stateful encoder` 來補充我們的方法，該 `encoder` 在生成過程中動態地重新導向編碼。
radar.title: Every Bit, Everywhere, All at Once: A Binomial Multibit LLM Watermark
radar.title_zh: 每個位元，同時遍佈各處：一種二項式多位元 LLM 浮水印

links.title: LongMemEval-V2: Evaluating Long-Term Agent Memory Toward Experienced Colleagues

primary_link.title: LongMemEval-V2: Evaluating Long-Term Agent Memory Toward Experienced Colleagues

radar.summary: Long-term memory is crucial for agents in specialized web environments, where success depends on recalling interface affordances, state dynamics, workflows, and recurring failure modes. radar.summary_zh: 長期記憶對於在專業 `web environments` 中的 `agents` 至關重要，在這些環境中，成功取決於回憶 `interface affordances`、`state dynamics`、`workflows` 以及重複出現的 `failure modes`。然而，現有的 `agent` 記憶基準主要關注 `user histories`、`short traces` 或 `downstream task success`，對於如何直接評估記憶系統是否有效內化環境特定經驗的問題仍未解決。為了彌補這一空白，我們引入了 `LongMemEval-V2 (LME-V2)`，這是一個針對評估 `agent` 長期記憶的基準。
radar.title: LongMemEval-V2: Evaluating Long-Term Agent Memory Toward Experienced Colleagues
radar.title_zh: LongMemEval-V2：評估智能體如同經驗豐富的同事般的長期記憶

primary_link.title: ORCE: Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence in Large Language Models

primary_link.title: Events as Triggers for Behavioral Diversity in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning

radar.summary_zh: 有效的 multi-agent 合作要求 agents 在任務條件演變時採取多樣化的行為——並在適當的時機這樣做。然而，當前促進這種多樣性的 Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) 框架仍然受到限制，因為它們將固定行為綁定到固定的 agent 身份。因此，它們不適用於 agent 需要在非常特定的時間點扮演不同角色的任務。我們認為，為了定義這些行為轉變

primary_link.title: Fill the GAP: A Granular Alignment Paradigm for Visual Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models

radar.title: Fill the GAP: A Granular Alignment Paradigm for Visual Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models

links.title: Learning Agentic Policy from Action Guidance

primary_link.title: Learning Agentic Policy from Action Guidance

top5.summary: Agentic reinforcement learning (RL) for Large Language Models (LLMs) critically depends on the exploration capability of the base policy, as training signals emerge only within its in-capability region. For tasks where the base policy cannot reach reward states, additional training or external guidance is needed to recover effective learning signals. Rather than relying on costly iterative supervised fine tuning (SFT), we exploit the abundant action data generated in everyday human interactions.
top5.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 的 Agentic reinforcement learning (RL) 極度依賴於 base policy 的探索能力，因為訓練訊號僅在其 in-capability region 內產生。對於 base policy 無法達到獎勵狀態的任務，需要額外的訓練或外部指導才能恢復有效的學習訊號。我們並非依賴成本高昂的 iterative supervised fine tuning (SFT)，而是利用日常人類互動中產生的大量動作數據。
top5.title: Learning Agentic Policy from Action Guidance
top5.title_zh: 從動作指導中學習 Agentic 策略

primary_link.title: On-Policy Self-Evolution via Failure Trajectories for Agentic Safety Alignment

top5.summary: Tool-using LLM agents fail through trajectories rather than only final responses, as they may execute unsafe tool calls, follow injected instructions, comply with harmful requests, or over-refuse benign tasks despite producing a seemingly safe answer.
